claim_text: Scoring formula has structural exposure bias (CAGR term = 108-182% of delta)
practical_implication: Never use raw score to compare strategies with different exposure levels
safe_wording: The scoring formula mechanically favors high-exposure strategies due to its 2.5x CAGR term
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: The scoring formula is fair and objective

claim_text: Engine differences are not a confounder (max divergence 7.7e-14%)
practical_implication: Both engines can be used interchangeably for comparison
safe_wording: The two backtest engines produce functionally identical results when configured equivalently
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: Engine differences explain the performance gap

claim_text: E0 has a signal-quality edge at equal sizing (+0.14 Sharpe at EntryVol_12)
practical_implication: Direction is plausible but not confirmed; do not rely on this as a proven advantage
safe_wording: Best available point estimate suggests E0 has a +0.14 Sharpe signal-quality advantage, but the 95% CI includes zero
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: E0's signal is proven superior to LATCH

claim_text: LATCH produces higher CAGR at 5% MDD budget (+1.2 pp)
practical_implication: Direction is plausible (P(>0)=77%) but not confirmed at 95% significance
safe_wording: Tentative evidence indicates LATCH/SM produces higher CAGR at low risk budgets
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: LATCH dominates E0 at low risk

claim_text: LATCH produces higher CAGR at 10% MDD budget (+2.3 pp)
practical_implication: Same as C04; advantage is directional not proven
safe_wording: Tentative evidence favors LATCH/SM at moderate risk budgets
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: LATCH is clearly better than E0 at 10% MDD

claim_text: E0 has higher native risk capacity (MDD ceiling 63% vs 11-15%)
practical_implication: E0 is the only candidate for high-risk mandates (MDD > 15%)
safe_wording: E0 is the only candidate that can fill high-risk drawdown budgets without leverage
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: LATCH can compete with E0 at any risk level

claim_text: LATCH complexity premium over SM is absent (Sharpe +0.003; P(>0)=55%)
practical_implication: SM should replace LATCH as the preferred low-exposure candidate
safe_wording: Unsupported by current evidence: LATCH's added complexity provides no measurable benefit over SM

claim_text: Crossover MDD budget is not stable (range 3%-26.5%)
practical_implication: Do not treat ~20% MDD crossover as a fixed boundary
safe_wording: The crossover MDD budget varies from 3% to 26.5% across time windows

claim_text: Start-date sensitivity: 2020-01 start reverses low-risk conclusions
practical_implication: The 2018 bear market is important for LATCH/SM's advantage over E0
safe_wording: Conclusions about low-risk advantage are sensitive to whether the 2018 bear market is included
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: The low-risk LATCH advantage is time-invariant

claim_text: P adds no unique non-dominated value
practical_implication: Deprioritize P; SM dominates on Sharpe (1.31 vs 1.24) and Calmar (0.93 vs 0.85)
safe_wording: P is dominated by SM on every major metric and should be deprioritized
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: P offers a valuable alternative to SM

claim_text: SM and LATCH are 99.8% concordant at defaults
practical_implication: The hysteretic regime in LATCH barely changes trading decisions at default parameters
safe_wording: SM and LATCH produce nearly identical trading decisions despite different regime logic
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: LATCH's hysteretic regime fundamentally changes the signal

claim_text: 35% of original score gap is sizing/exposure not signal
practical_implication: Always decompose signal vs sizing before drawing conclusions
safe_wording: 35% of the score delta between E0 and LATCH comes from sizing/exposure confounders
unsafe_overstatement_to_avoid: The full score gap reflects signal quality
